NULLED:NCT03887455.name: Clarity-AD
NULLED:NCT03887455.group: Early AD (MCI or mild dementia, amyloid+)

allOutcomes.title: 18-month CDR-Sum of Boxes change (adjusted mean difference vs placebo)

NULLED:NCT03887455.snippet: Source: van Dyck CH et al. NEJM 2023;388:9-21 (Clarity-AD).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: van Dyck CH et al. NEJM 2023;388:9-21, CONSORT
evidence.text: 1,795 adults aged 50-90 with early Alzheimer disease (MCI or mild dementia) and amyloid-PET or CSF confirmation were randomized 1:1 to lecanemab 10 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks (n=898) or placebo (n=897) for 18 months.

evidence.label: Primary — CDR-SB at 18 months
evidence.source: van Dyck CH et al. NEJM 2023, Table 2
evidence.text: CDR-SB increased by 1.21 with lecanemab vs 1.66 with placebo (adjusted mean difference -0.45; 95% CI -0.67 to -0.23; P<0.001). Represents 27% slowing of decline.

evidence.label: ARIA Safety
evidence.source: van Dyck CH et al. NEJM 2023
evidence.text: ARIA-E: 12.6% (113/898) lecanemab vs 1.7% (17/897) placebo. ARIA-H: 17.3% vs 9.0%. Symptomatic ARIA: 2.8% lecanemab. ApoE ε4 homozygotes had highest risk.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; max age 90 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Core Study: Inclusion Criteria~Diagnosis: Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) due to Alzheimer's disease - intermediate likelihood:~* Meet the National Institute of Aging - Alzheimer's Association (NIA-AA) core clinical criteria for MCI due to Alzheimer's disease - intermediate likelihood~* Have a global Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR) score of 0.5 and CDR Memory Box score of 0.5 or greater at Screening and Baseline~* Report a history of subjective memory decline with gradual onset and slow progress...

NULLED:NCT04437511.name: TRAILBLAZER-ALZ2
NULLED:NCT04437511.group: Early symptomatic AD with amyloid+ and tau PET (low-medium tau stratum primary)

allOutcomes.title: 18-month CDR-Sum of Boxes change (combined-pop adjusted mean difference vs placebo)

NULLED:NCT04437511.snippet: Source: Sims JR et al. JAMA 2023;330:512-527 (TRAILBLAZER-ALZ2).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Sims JR et al. JAMA 2023;330:512-527, CONSORT
evidence.text: 1,736 adults with early symptomatic AD and amyloid + tau PET confirmation (pre-specified low-medium tau primary population and combined population) randomized 1:1 to donanemab 1400 mg IV every 4 weeks (n=860) vs placebo (n=876) for up to 76 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary — CDR-SB at 76 weeks (combined population)
evidence.source: Sims JR et al. JAMA 2023, Table 2
evidence.text: CDR-SB change at 76 weeks (combined population): 1.72 donanemab vs 2.42 placebo; adjusted mean difference -0.70 (95% CI -0.95 to -0.45; P<0.001). 29% slowing of disease progression.

evidence.label: ARIA Safety
evidence.text: ARIA-E: 24.0% (205/853 evaluable) donanemab vs 2.1% (16/874) placebo. Symptomatic ARIA: 6.1%. Three deaths considered treatment-related.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 60 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Gradual and progressive change in memory function reported by participants or informants for ≥ 6 months~* MMSE score of 20 to 28 (inclusive) at baseline~* Meet 18F flortaucipir PET scan (central read) criteria - does not apply to safety cohort~* Meet 18F florbetapir PET scan (central read) criteria~* Have a study partner who will provide written informed consent to participate~Exclusion Criteria:~* Contraindication to MRI or PET scans~* Current treatment with immunoglobuli...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: Female 488, Female 501, Female 989. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT02477800.name: EMERGE
NCT02477800.group: Aducanumab 10 mg/kg Q4W IV | Anti-amyloid mAb (EMERGE POSITIVE pivotal — CDR-SB MD vs placebo at wk 78)
NCT02477800.snippet: Source: Budd Haeberlein S et al. J Prev Alzheimers Dis 2022;9:197-210 (EMERGE pooled with ENGAGE).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Budd Haeberlein S et al. J Prev Alzheimers Dis 2022
evidence.text: 1,638 EMERGE participants (early AD, MCI or mild dementia stage, amyloid-PET +) randomized 1:1:1 to aducanumab high-dose (10 mg/kg), low-dose (6 mg/kg), or placebo. Analysis uses high-dose vs placebo per regulatory submission (n=547 high-dose vs n=548 placebo).

evidence.label: Primary — CDR-SB at 78 weeks (high-dose)
evidence.source: Budd Haeberlein S et al. J Prev Alzheimers Dis 2022, Table
evidence.text: EMERGE primary met: 22% slowing on CDR-SB at week 78 with aducanumab high-dose vs placebo (adjusted mean difference -0.39; 95% CI -0.79 to 0.00; P=0.05). Parallel ENGAGE trial did NOT meet primary. FDA accelerated approval 2021; later withdrawn by manufacturer 2024.

evidence.label: ARIA Safety
evidence.source: Salloway S et al. JAMA Neurol 2022 (EMERGE+ENGAGE safety)
evidence.text: ARIA-E: 35% high-dose aducanumab (26% in EMERGE alone) vs 2.7% placebo. Symptomatic ARIA: 24% of high-dose ARIA-E cases. ApoE ε4 carriers at highest risk.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Must meet all of the following clinical criteria for MCI due to AD or mild AD and must have:~* A Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR)-Global Score of 0.5.~* Objective evidence of cognitive impairment at screening~* An MMSE score between 24 and 30 (inclusive)~* Must have a positive amyloid Positron Emission Tomography (PET) scan~* Must consent to apolipoprotein E (ApoE) genotyping~* If using drugs to treat symptoms related to AD, doses must be stable for at least 8 weeks prio...

NCT02484547.name: ENGAGE
NCT02484547.group: Aducanumab 10 mg/kg Q4W IV | Anti-amyloid mAb (EMERGE pair; NEGATIVE pivotal CDR-SB MD; pooled aducanumab efficacy attenuated by ENGAGE)

allOutcomes.title: CDR-SB change at 78 weeks aducanumab 10 mg/kg high-dose vs placebo (slope ratio RR-equivalent; ENGAGE failed primary vs EMERGE positive)

NCT02484547.snippet: Source: Budd Haeberlein S et al. J Prev Alzheimers Dis 2022;9:197-210 (EMERGE + ENGAGE). ENGAGE failed primary endpoint; pooled with EMERGE attenuates aducanumab efficacy toward null.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Must meet all of the following clinical criteria for MCI due to AD or mild AD and must have:~* A Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR)-Global Score of 0.5.~* Objective evidence of cognitive impairment at screening~* An MMSE score between 24 and 30 (inclusive)~* Must have a positive amyloid Positron Emission Tomography (PET) scan~* Must consent to apolipoprotein E (ApoE) genotyping~* If using drugs to treat symptoms related to AD, doses must be stable for at least 8 weeks prio...

title: RapidMeta Neurology | Anti-Amyloid Antibodies in Alzheimer’s Disease v1.0
